title: Understanding Rotational Kinetic Energy
text: Rotational kinetic energy is given by the formula \( KE_{rot} $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} I \omega**2 \), where \( I \) is the moment of inertia and \( \omega \) is the angular velocity.

title: Calculate the Moment of Inertia
text: The moment of inertia \( I \) for a solid sphere is \( \frac{2}{5} M $$R^2$$ \). Use the given mass \( M $$=$$ 5.97*10**{24} \) kg and radius \( R $$=$$ $$6.37\times{10}^6$$ \) $$m$$ to calculate \( I \).

title: Calculate the Moment of Inertia
text: Substitute the values into the formula \( I $$=$$ \frac{2}{5} M $$R^2$$ \).

title: Calculate Angular Velocity
text: The angular velocity \( \omega \) is given by \( \omega $$=$$ \frac{2\pi}{T} \), where \( T \) is the period of rotation. For Earth, \( T $$=$$ $$8.64\times{10}^4$$ \) seconds.

title: Calculate Angular Velocity
text: Substitute the period \( T $$=$$ $$8.64\times{10}^4$$ \) s into the formula \( \omega $$=$$ \frac{2\pi}{T} \).

title: Combine to Find Rotational Kinetic Energy
text: Now that you have \( I \) and \( \omega \), substitute them into the formula \( KE_{rot} $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} I \omega**2 \) to find the rotational kinetic energy.
